pip-compile.description: The only pip surface this fork owns: the formatting venv the check-formatting job installs. It is a `uv pip compile --generate-hashes` lockfile, so it must go through the pip-compile manager (which re-runs uv to regenerate the --hash= lines) — pip_requirements would bump the == pin and leave the hashes stale, breaking `pip install --require-hashes`. The name is non-standard, so match it explicitly; includePaths keeps the dozens of upstream requirements*.txt files out.

packageRules.description: solx-ci-runner is a private GHCR image pinned by digest in the workflow's container:. It's rebuilt and re-pinned in NomicFoundation/solx, not here; Renovate can't read the private package anyway.

Formatting venv (black/darker/PyGithub): security-only. requirements_formatting.txt.in is an upstream LLVM file, so routine version bumps here are a fork divergence that the next full LLVM rebase resets or conflicts — near-zero value for one formatting job (see PR #111, where darker 1.7.3 broke against the pinned black). vulnerabilityAlerts/osvVulnerabilityAlerts override enabled:false, so CVE fixes still open PRs.
